verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal dual lipid modifications (myristoylation + palmitoylation). It has no transmembrane domain, no signal peptide, and no extracellular domain. No binder of any modality can access it from the extracellular face. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet display under cancer, apoptosis, ICD, infection, or activation states; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of outer-leaflet display in any tissue/lineage, including germline; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — LYN is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — PM pool is real but strictly on the cytoplasmic face; endomembrane/nuclear pools are also intracellular; (5) stable_surface_attachment — LYN is not secreted and has no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent link to a TM partner from the extracellular side. Its association with BCR/FcεRI occurs on the cytoplasmic face. Verdict: inner_leaflet_anchored.
